content.full_text:
RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

June 28, 1995

Jim Beam
Jim Beam for Assembly
3745 West Chapman Avenue, Ste. 203
Orange, CA  92668

Re:  Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-95-205

Dear Mr. Beam:

You have requested assistance concerning the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/  Section
83114 and Regulation 18329 provide that any person may request
advice concerning his or her own duties under the Act. Because
you have not indicated that you are an authorized representative
of Orange National Bank, we cannot provide advice to the bank at
your request.  However, we will provide you with the following
informal assistance:2/

You have asked what reporting requirements the Bank of Orange
would have if it were to make a loan to your campaign committee.

A loan made by a commercial lending institution in the
ordinary course of business is not a contribution and, therefore,
is not required to be reported by the lending institution.

_____________________________
1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/  Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814  (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-95-205
Page 2


     The Act requires persons or combinations of persons who
contribute $10,000 or more in a calendar year to state and
local candidates and committees to file periodic campaign
reports disclosing contributions and expenditures made.
(Sections 82013(c), 84200-84216.5.) In Section 84216, the
Act states that:

     .... a loan received by a candidate or committee is a
contribution unless the loan is received from a
commercial lending institution in the ordinary course of
business, or it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that it is not made for political
purposes.

     If you have additional questions, please do not hesitate
to contact me at (916) 322-5660.

                                   Sincerely,

                                   [signature]
                                   Trish Mayer
                                   Political Reform Consultant

cc:  Larry Sallinger
     Orange National Bank

sections.question: What reporting requirements would the Bank of Orange have if it were to make a loan to your campaign committee?
sections.conclusion: A loan made by a commercial lending institution in the ordinary course of business is not a contribution and, therefore, is not required to be reported by the lending institution. A loan received by a candidate or committee is a contribution unless the loan is received from a commercial lending institution in the ordinary course of business, or it is clear from the surrounding circumstances that it is not made for political purposes.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked what reporting requirements apply if a commercial bank makes a loan to his campaign committee.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Bank loans made in the ordinary course of business are not reportable contributions under the Political Reform Act, though loans from other sources are contributions unless clearly not made for political purposes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard informal advice letter format with clear question and conclusion sections. Document declines to provide advice to the bank itself but provides informal assistance to the requestor. Contains statutory citations and regulatory references.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What reporting requirements would the Bank of Orange have if it were to make a loan to your campaign committee?

CONCLUSION: A loan made by a commercial lending institution in the ordinary course of business is not a contribution and, therefore, is not required to be reported by the lending institution. A loan received by a candidate or committee is a contribution unless the loan is received from a commercial lending institution in the ordinary course of business, or it is clear from the surrounding circumstances that it is not made for political purposes.

embedding.summary: FPPC provides informal advice to Jim Beam regarding campaign loan reporting requirements, clarifying that loans from commercial lending institutions in the ordinary course of business are not reportable contributions under the Political Reform Act.
